methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $70M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 6 same-sector + same-stage peers in Robotics & Manufacturing. Range: $300M - $10.0B.

private.name: 1X Technologies
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series B), post-money $10B

private.name: Bedrock Robotics
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series B), post-money $1.75B

private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series B), post-money $500M+

private.name: Fairmat
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series B), post-money $350M

private.name: GrayMatter Robotics
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series B), post-money $300M+

data.narrative:
Dusty Robotics, a Series B robotics and manufacturing company founded by Tessa Lau, has raised $70M+ to date. The valuation analysis draws on a comparable universe of 9 peers—1 public company and 8 private robotics firms at the same development stage, including 1X Technologies, Bedrock Robotics, Dexterity, Collaborative Robotics, and Fairmat.

Two valuation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis uses the $70M raised as a proxy base value and applies a 1.9x step-up multiplier typical for Series B advancement, yielding $133M. Peer-median analysis across six same-sector, same-stage comparables produces a median valuation of $1.1B, with a range spanning $300M to $10.0B. The wide peer dispersion reflects heterogeneity within robotics valuations at this stage.

Triangulating both methods produces a valuation range of $113M (low) to $1.2B (high), with a median estimate of $604M. The substantial spread between stage-progression and peer-median results reflects two distinct signals: capital deployed relative to typical dilution patterns versus observed market pricing for similar-stage robotics ventures. Key uncertainties include revenue scale, customer concentration, and technical differentiation within the peer set, particularly given the tenfold valuation gap between the lowest and highest comparables. A more granular analysis of peer revenue multiples or capital efficiency would be needed to narrow this range materially.
